primary_sources.use: append-only Merkle consistency verification and split-view trust boundary

finding: A compact consistency proof can establish append-only relation between two roots, but authentication and equivocation detection require signed checkpoint statements plus comparison across retained witness observations.

claim_states.observed: R-008 explicitly left checkpoint authentication and split-view detection unresolved.
claim_states.implemented: Ed25519 checkpoint signing, trusted-signer verification, witness observation records, rollback classification, proof binding checks, and equal-size divergent-root equivocation detection.
claim_states.inferred: Independent witnesses that retain signed checkpoints can produce portable equivocation evidence when the same signer presents different roots for the same tree size.
claim_states.proposed: Run exact-commit tests in CI and add a multi-witness quorum policy with key rotation and revocation semantics.
claim_states.unresolved: Private-key custody, signer authorization lifecycle, witness independence, quorum thresholds, freshness policy, and provider CI execution.

verification.local_execution: blocked because the runtime could not resolve raw.githubusercontent.com; no test-pass claim is made

falsification_route: reject signatures that fail Ed25519 verification or use an untrusted signer identity
classify equal tree sizes with divergent roots as equivocation
reject a newer observation with a smaller tree size as rollback
reject unequal checkpoints without a valid consistency proof bound to both signed roots
do not infer global non-equivocation from one witness or one internally consistent history
limits: Signatures authenticate possession of a key, not organizational authorization, payload truth, witness independence, freshness, or global consistency.
